title: CLI /send command: chunk file and broadcast
description:
## What
Add /send <path> command to the CLI chat loop in cli/src/commands.rs. Reads file, chunks into 48KB blocks, sends FileHeader then FileChunk messages via MLS broadcast.

## Algorithm
1. In the chat input loop, add: if let Some(path_str) = line.strip_prefix('/send ') { ... continue; }
2. Path handling: let path = std::path::Path::new(path_str.trim()); validate it exists and is a file.
3. Read file: let bytes = tokio::fs::read(&path).await? - handle errors with println!.
4. Enforce client-side limit: const FILE_MAX_BYTES: usize = 10 * 1024 * 1024; if bytes.len() > FILE_MAX_BYTES { println!('[send] file too large ({} bytes > 10 MB limit)', bytes.len()); continue; }
5. Compute SHA-256: use sha2::Sha256 + Digest trait; sha256_hex = bytes.iter().map(|b| format!({'b:02x'})).collect().
6. MIME type: simple extension lookup - match path.extension().and_then(|e| e.to_str()) { Some('jpg'|'jpeg') => 'image/jpeg', Some('png') => 'image/png', Some('pdf') => 'application/pdf', Some('txt') => 'text/plain', Some('zip') => 'application/zip', _ => 'application/octet-stream' }.
7. transfer_id = Uuid::new_v4(); chunk_size = 48 * 1024; chunks: Vec<&[u8]> = bytes.chunks(chunk_size).collect(); total_chunks = chunks.len() as u32.
8. Send header: let header = ClearMessage::FileHeader { transfer_id, name: path.file_name().unwrap().to_string_lossy().into_owned(), size_bytes: bytes.len() as u64, sha256_hex, total_chunks, mime_type }; serialize and MLS-encrypt and pad as normal chat message.
9. For each chunk (seq 0..total_chunks): let chunk_msg = ClearMessage::FileChunk { transfer_id, seq: seq as u32, data: chunk.to_vec() }; serialize, pad to 65536 bucket, MLS encrypt (or plaintext if no MLS), send via BROADCAST. Print progress every 10 chunks: println!('[send] {}/{} chunks', seq+1, total_chunks).
10. After all chunks: println!('[send] sent {} ({} bytes, {} chunks)', name, size, total_chunks).

## Defensive programming
- Validate path is a file (not directory, not symlink) before reading
- Never log file content or sha256_hex (only metadata: name, size, chunk count)
- Check tx.send() result for each chunk; break on connection loss
- Path component: use path.file_name() to extract just the filename for display
- Chunk data: call messages::pad(&mls_ciphertext_or_plaintext); if pad returns Err, skip sending that chunk and report error
- No unwrap() in production code

## Tests
Not a standalone testable function; tested via E2E bead.
acceptance_criteria: cargo build -p nie-cli succeeds; /send command is parseable in the input loop
close_reason: CLI /send command: reads file, SHA-256, MIME detection, sends FileHeader+FileChunk via MLS broadcast, progress display, 10MB client-side cap
